title: Quantitation of aristolochic acid using high performance liquid chromatography with photodiode array detection
author: D. V. Singh
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Central Institute of Medicinal and Aromatic Plants, Lucknow-226 015, India 

  E-mail:  cimap@satyam.net.in       Fax  : 91-0522-342666 

  Manuscript received 11 August 2000. revised 26 March 2001, accepted 31 March 2001  

 A rapid, sensitive and reproducible HPLC method with photodiode array detection for quantitative determination of aristolochic acid in plant sample is described. The procedure involves the extraction of aristolochic acid with methanol and chromatographic separation with a mobile phase of acetonitrile-water-trifluoroacetic acid-tetrahydrofuran. The average recovery of aristolochic acid is 97.8%. The minimum detectable concentration is 0.10 µg/injection of 5 µl injection volume.
